name: Steel MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Steel MCP server enabling AI agents to use Steel's cloud browser automation platform — controlling headless browsers, scraping web pages, filling forms, handling authentication flows, taking screenshots, and executing complex multi-step web interactions. Steel provides managed browser infrastructure with built-in anti-bot bypass, session management, and scalable browser pools.
use_cases: Automating complex web interactions requiring browser execution from task automation agents
Scraping JavaScript-rendered web content from data collection agents
Handling web authentication flows and maintaining browser sessions from workflow agents
Taking screenshots and generating PDFs from web pages from documentation agents
Filling web forms and interacting with web applications from process automation agents
Testing web application user flows from QA automation agents
not_for: Simple static HTML scraping (use lightweight HTTP fetch tools for static pages)
Teams who self-host Playwright/Puppeteer (use Playwright MCP directly for self-hosted automation)
High-volume bulk scraping without verifying site ToS compliance
best_when: An agent needs to interact with JavaScript-heavy web applications in a managed, scalable browser environment — Steel handles browser infrastructure so agents can focus on task logic.
avoid_when: You already have Playwright or Puppeteer set up locally — use direct Playwright MCP integration for self-hosted browser automation.

auth.notes: Steel API key required. Set STEEL_API_KEY environment variable. Get key from steel.dev account. API key scopes browser session access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Steel free tier with limited browser minutes per month
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-browser-minute with volume discounts; startup and enterprise plans available
pricing.notes: Usage-based pricing by browser compute time. Free tier available for development. Monitor usage in agent loops — browser automation is compute-intensive.

performance.rate_limits: Browser session concurrency limits by plan; rate limits on API calls

known_agent_gotchas: Browser session costs accumulate — implement session timeouts and budget monitoring
Web page changes break automation — implement robust selectors and fallback strategies
Anti-bot measures may still block some sites despite Steel's bypass capabilities
Session state management is important — Steel manages browser state between calls
Official MCP from steel-dev — good quality and documentation
ToS compliance is user's responsibility — verify target sites allow automated access
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS. API key auth. Steel manages browser infrastructure in US. Web interactions may involve credentials/sensitive data — ensure secure session handling. Keep STEEL_API_KEY secure.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. API key. Steel cloud infrastructure (US). Browser interactions may involve sensitive site credentials. Keep API key secure. ToS compliance responsibility on user.
